entries.title: Life Critical Rules
entries.headline: A safety training system built to survive operations scrutiny, not just look cinematic.
entries.summary: Content Co-op turned CITGO's Life Critical Rules into a serialized training package built for repeat use, field comprehension, and stakeholder confidence.
entries.mandate: Package mandatory safety guidance into a format crews would actually watch while keeping the language credible for operations and HSE review.
entries.execution: The series combines animated instruction, live footage, and episodic packaging so each rule can stand alone or work as part of a broader training system.
entries.outcome: The result reads like a durable communications system: organized, watchable, and usable across a real rollout.
proofPoints: Balances operations clarity with premium finish

review.notes: Cover and supporting stills align to safety/operations context.

gallery.alt: Fire and safety gear arranged as proof imagery for CITGO Life Critical Rules
Industrial control room still representing operations scrutiny in the CITGO safety series
Safety collage still used as proof imagery for the CITGO training package

entries.title: CERAWeek Coverage
entries.headline: Conference coverage built to preserve signal after the event, not disappear into the feed.
entries.summary: Executive interviews, summit moments, and post-event edits were shaped into a durable conference package for two enterprise energy brands.
entries.mandate: Capture conference access, executive presence, and brand relevance in a way that remained useful after the event week ended.
entries.execution: The coverage approach combined interview structure, event texture, and recap packaging so the work could support thought leadership and follow-through.
entries.outcome: The finished pieces turn conference access into proof for marketing, communications, and leadership teams instead of leaving it as disposable social content.

review.notes: Conference stills are now event-specific and no longer padded with unrelated industrial photos.

gallery.alt: Speaker on stage at CERAWeek energy conference

entries.title: Adaptive Stainless
entries.headline: Fabrication capability framed with enough discipline to feel premium, not generic industrial stock.
entries.summary: A shop-floor brand film built around precision fabrication, process clarity, and the people behind the work.
entries.mandate: Turn custom metalwork and fabrication capability into a brand story that felt clean, modern, and credible to industrial buyers.
entries.execution: The edit leans on machinery detail, process rhythm, and a controlled visual language rather than broad claims or overproduced corporate messaging.
entries.outcome: The final film reads as industrial proof: exacting, confident, and specific to the work itself.
proofPoints: Useful for sales and positioning conversations

review.notes: Single proof still is specific to the fabrication environment.

gallery.alt: Machinist operating a CNC machine during Adaptive Stainless production

entries.title: Accurate Meter & Supply
entries.headline: A precision-measurement business framed like a premium capability, not a commodity vendor.
entries.summary: Facilities, field operations, and leadership context were shaped into a positioning film that makes technical capability feel credible and commercially sharp.
entries.mandate: Show both engineering seriousness and commercial polish for a business serving energy-sector clients with precision equipment and expertise.
entries.execution: The film combines facility coverage, field reality, and leadership messaging into one coherent story instead of splitting technical proof from brand narrative.
entries.outcome: The piece gives the company a stronger sales-facing story and a visual language that supports premium positioning.
proofPoints: Facility and field context in one narrative
Executive message integrated into the proof

review.notes: Only project-specific stills remain in the gallery.

gallery.alt: Proof frame from the Accurate Meter brand film
Industrial equipment still from Accurate Meter work
Close-up proof still from Accurate Meter brand film

entries.title: Kodiak Gas Services
entries.headline: Recruitment work aimed at real operators, with the field reality doing the selling.
entries.summary: The film centers actual work, equipment, and crew context so recruitment messaging feels grounded in the job rather than abstract employer-brand language.
entries.mandate: Attract skilled operators and technicians without flattening the work into generic culture messaging.
entries.execution: Field operations, equipment detail, and crew presence carry the narrative so prospects can quickly read what the job and company actually feel like.
entries.outcome: The project gives recruitment conversations a sharper front door and stronger credibility with technical talent.
deliverables: Culture story grounded in the work

review.notes: Supporting stills are grounded in field context and marked as shared environment where client-specific naming is unavailable.

gallery.alt: Kodiak field crew in hard hats
Desert tank and energy landscape from Kodiak recruiting work
